commit.message:
feat: log WebSocket frames by default, -ws-no-log to suppress

After a WebSocket upgrade (101 Switching Protocols), httputil's bidirectional
copy bypasses ModifyResponse entirely. To give operators visibility into WS
traffic without buffering or payload modification, frame headers are now parsed
and logged as they stream through the proxy.

Implementation:
- wsLoggingTransport: wraps the HTTP transport; intercepts 101 responses and
  replaces resp.Body with a wsLoggingConn
- wsLoggingConn: stateful io.ReadWriteCloser wrapper that parses WS frame
  headers in both directions with zero buffering (bytes pass through unchanged)
- wsFrameParseState: per-direction header accumulator; handles arbitrary chunk
  sizes across Read/Write calls; supports short (≤125 B), 16-bit, and 64-bit
  payload lengths, masked frames, control frames, and fragmented frames
- Logger.LogWSFrame: logs opcode name, conn ID, direction, payload length,
  masked and fragment flags; no-op when logWS=false

Log output example:
  WS↕  conn#1 established
  WS↑  conn#1  op=text    len=42 [masked]
  WS↓  conn#1  op=text    len=38
  WS↓  conn#1  op=ping    len=0

Flags:
  (default)    WS frames logged to stderr
  -ws-no-log   suppress WS frame logging

commit.message:
`clang-tidy`: resolve `bugprone-macro-parentheses` (#500)

Warnings were confined to two header files and determined to be false
positives—parentheses around uses of parameters in C++ code generation
macros are not only superfluous, but disastrous for successful
compilation.

Macros are now protected from this check _en bloc_ due to the
impossibility of threading perl-line `NOLINT` statements in macro
definitions with line continuations.

commit.message:
fix: prevent input focus loss when typing in recipe edit/new forms

IngredientRow and StepRow were defined as const arrow functions inside
the parent component body. Every keystroke triggered a re-render, React
saw a new component reference, and unmounted/remounted the input — losing
focus after each character typed.

Fix: move both components to module-level named functions so React
preserves their identity across re-renders.

Affects both /recipes/[id]/edit and /recipes/new pages.

commit.message:
Improve teams-bot-infra skill: optional endpoint, auto .env, and error handling (#80)

* Fix Azure CLI detection on Windows using cmd /c wrapper

Root cause: execFile() cannot spawn .cmd files directly on Windows,
resulting in spawn EINVAL error. Previous attempts used shell:true
which introduced command injection vulnerabilities.

Solution: Use cmd.exe with /c flag to execute az.cmd on Windows,
which is secure (blocks injection) and cross-platform compatible.

- Windows: cmd /c az.cmd <args>
- Unix: az <args>

Security: Verified that special characters (&&, ||, ;) are treated
as literal arguments, not executed as shell commands.

Fixes #28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add teams-bot-infra agent skill for Teams bot infrastructure management

Implement production-grade agent skill following the 5-phase development lifecycle:
- Phase 1: Discovered and validated workflow via manual execution
- Phase 2: Designed modular architecture (single skill covering create, configure, troubleshoot)
- Phase 3: Implemented SKILL.md with verification checkpoints and error recovery
- Phase 4: Validated against real workflow (leveraged Phase 1 testing)
- Phase 5: Packaged for distribution with installation documentation

Skill features:
- Complete bot creation workflow (CLI install → auth → create → verify)
- Verification checkpoints requiring evidence at each step
- Common operations (update endpoint, view apps, update CLI)
- Error recovery (auth required, sideload disabled)
- Resource links (Teams SDK, devtunnels, Microsoft docs)

Scope: Bot infrastructure only (excludes bot code development and hosting)

Installation: npx skills add heyitsaamir/teamscli --skill teams-bot-infra

Resolves #37

**`src/jarify/generator.py`**

- `schema_sql` — puts the opening `(` on its own line when in pretty
mode, consistent with the existing CTE layout rule.
- `schema_columns_sql` — splits column definitions from table-level
constraints, computes column-name and type alignment widths, renders
columns padded to align all type and constraint tokens, and inserts a
blank line before table-level constraints (`PRIMARY KEY`, `UNIQUE`,
`CHECK`, etc.) when present.
- `columndef_sql` — honours the two alignment widths stored during
`schema_columns_sql`; falls back to default behaviour outside `CREATE
TABLE` (e.g. `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`).
- `datatype_sql` — lowercases all data type names (`text`, `int`,
`timestamp`, `decimal`, etc.) while leaving SQL statement keywords
(`SELECT`, `FROM`, `NOT NULL`, `DEFAULT`, `PRIMARY KEY`, etc.)
uppercase.

**`tests/fixtures/create_table/`**

Three new fixture pairs:

| Fixture | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `basic` | Full DDL: mixed custom/built-in types, `DEFAULT`, nullable
columns, table-level `PRIMARY KEY` |
| `simple_no_constraints` | Plain table with no constraints; verifies no
blank line is added |
| `inline_primary_key` | Column-level `PRIMARY KEY` constraint; verifies
no blank line is added |

**`tests/fixtures/duckdb/type_cast.expected.sql`** — updated to
lowercase types (`int`, `text`, `boolean`).

**`docs/sql-style-guide.md`** — adds `CREATE TABLE` layout rule and
updates the Keywords section to document the keyword-uppercase /
type-lowercase split.

Resolves #26

commit.message:
feat: RFC 9535 compliant JSONPath service with sort extension functions (#129)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement RFC 9535 compliant JsonPathService

- Add IJsonPathService interface in Core/Abstractions
- Add JsonPathService with full RFC 9535 support:
  - Name selectors (dot notation and bracket notation with quoted strings)
  - Index selectors (including negative indices)
  - Wildcard selectors ($.*  and $[*])
  - Slice selectors ($[1:3], $[::2], $[1:5:2])
  - Descendant segments ($..foo, $..[*])
  - Multiple selectors in one bracket ($[0,2,'name'])
  - Filter selectors with logical/comparison expressions
  - RFC 9535 built-in functions (length, count, match, search, value)
- Refactor SyntaxPathFilter to delegate to JsonPathService
- Register IJsonPathService in App.axaml.cs DI
- Add 78 comprehensive tests covering all RFC 9535 features

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/e00a6310-abf7-46b7-be8e-ce07beb24bec

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove static service instantiation from SyntaxPathFilter

- SyntaxPathFilter.TryTransform now accepts IJsonPathService as parameter
- FilteredSyntaxViewer exposes JsonPath property (injectable, defaults to new JsonPathService)
- Update SyntaxPathFilterTests to pass service explicitly
- Improve test assertions for DescendantIndex and ExistenceTestOnObject tests

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/e00a6310-abf7-46b7-be8e-ce07beb24bec

* feat: add sort/sort_asc/sort_desc JSONPath extension functions

- Add IJsonPathService.TryQuery() for runtime error propagation
- Introduce IPathStep interface with SortStep record alongside Segment
- Parser detects .sort(), .sort_asc(), .sort_desc() function calls
  - sort()/sort_asc() ascending; sort_desc() descending
  - Sort expression (property name) required for object arrays
  - Sort expression forbidden for primitive arrays
  - Descendant sort ($..sort_asc()) is a parse error
- Numeric, string and mixed-type comparison via SortKeyComparer (nulls last)
- SyntaxPathFilter uses TryQuery to surface runtime sort errors in filter bar
- 18 new tests covering all sort behaviors and error cases

* fix: sort after wildcard/slice selectors flattens then sorts correctly

When [*] or a slice selector expands an array into individual elements,
the sort step now detects it's in "flat-list mode" (no node is itself an
array) and treats the whole nodes list as one virtual array to sort.

Paths like $..items[*].sort(price) and $.items[0:3].sort(v) now work.
